title: Masala Dosa
description: Crispy rice and lentil crepe filled with spiced potato. South Indian breakfast classic.

preparation.instruction: Soak rice and urad dal for dosa batter.
tips: This is the longest step - plan ahead. Longer soaking (4-8 hours) helps with fermentation
subSteps: Measure rice and urad dal separately.
Rinse both under running water until water runs clear.
Soak rice in water for 4 hours.
Soak urad dal in separate water for 4 hours.
Keep them separate - do not mix until grinding.
Boil potatoes until completely soft (10-15 minutes).
Peel potatoes while warm and keep aside.
Chop onion into small pieces.
Slit green chillies lengthwise.
Measure out mustard seeds, cumin seeds, turmeric, and salt.
Pluck fresh curry leaves from stems.
Keep ghee ready in a bowl.

steps.instruction: Prepare dosa batter by grinding rice and dal.
tips: Fermented batter gives crispy dosas. You can skip fermentation but crispy texture may vary.
subSteps: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 4 hours.
Drain water completely.
Grind urad dal first until it becomes light and fluffy (about 10 minutes).
Add rice gradually and grind until smooth batter forms.
Mix in salt and let batter ferment overnight or 6-8 hours.
The batter should almost double in volume.
Boil potatoes until soft and peel.
Mash potatoes lightly, keeping some chunks.
Heat ghee in a pan, add mustard seeds and let them pop.
Add curry leaves, green chillies, and onions.
Add mashed potatoes, turmeric, and salt.
Cook for 2 minutes and mix well.

steps.instruction: Cook crispy dosas on a griddle.
tips: Use a spreader or the back of ladle to spread batter thin. First dosa might be imperfect — that's normal!
subSteps: Heat a dosa griddle or non-stick pan on high heat.
Pour a ladleful of batter in the center.
Immediately spread in circular motions to make thin crepe.
Drizzle ghee on edges.
When the bottom is golden, add a spoonful of filling.
Fold into triangle or roll and serve hot.
Serve with sambar and coconut chutney.

seo.metaTitle: Masala Dosa Recipe — Crispy South Indian Breakfast
seo.metaDescription: Make crispy masala dosa at home with detailed step-by-step instructions for batter and filling.
